channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Enhancing Safety of Large Language Models via Embedding Space Separation
item.description: arXiv:2603.20206v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have achieved impressive capabilities, yet ensuring their safety against harmful prompts remains a critical challenge. Recent work has revealed that the latent representations (embeddings) of harmful and safe queries in LL...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, large language model, fine-tuning, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Children's Intelligence Tests Pose Challenges for MLLMs? KidGym: A 2D Grid-Based Reasoning Benchmark for MLLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.20209v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) combine the linguistic strengths of LLMs with the ability to process multimodal data, enbaling them to address a broader range of visual tasks. Because MLLMs aim at more general, human-like competence than lang...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, large language model, multimodal, API, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Fast-Slow Thinking RM: Efficient Integration of Scalar and Generative Reward Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.20212v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reward models (RMs) are critical for aligning Large Language Models via Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF). While Generative Reward Models (GRMs) achieve superior accuracy through chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning, they incur substantial...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, ICL, arxiv, chain-of-thought, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Multi-Agent Debate with Memory Masking
item.description: arXiv:2603.20215v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have recently demonstrated impressive capabilities in reasoning tasks. Currently, mainstream LLM reasoning frameworks predominantly focus on scaling up inference-time sampling to enhance performance. In particular, among a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, large language model, arxiv, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Expected Reward Prediction, with Applications to Model Routing
item.description: arXiv:2603.20217v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reward models are a standard tool to score responses from LLMs. Reward models are built to rank responses to a fixed prompt sampled from a single model, for example to choose the best of n sampled responses. In this paper, we study whether scores from...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, arxiv, model, tool, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An experimental study of KV cache reuse strategies in chunk-level caching systems
item.description: arXiv:2603.20218v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation improves large language models' accuracy by adding relevant retrieved text to the prompt. Chunk level caching (CLC) accelerates inference by precomputing KV caches for these retrieved chunks and reusing them. However, th...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, large language model, arxiv, model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Thinking into the Future: Latent Lookahead Training for Transformers
item.description: arXiv:2603.20219v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autoregressive language models trained with next-token prediction generate text by sampling one discrete token at a time. Although very scalable, this objective forces the model to commit at every step, preventing it from exploring or reflecting upon ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, transformer, model, RAG, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AgenticGEO: A Self-Evolving Agentic System for Generative Engine Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2603.20213v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative search engines represent a transition from traditional ranking-based retrieval to Large Language Model (LLM)-based synthesis, transforming optimization goals from ranking prominence towards content inclusion. Generative Engine Optimization ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, ICL, arxiv, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ProMAS: Proactive Error Forecasting for Multi-Agent Systems Using Markov Transition Dynamics
item.description: arXiv:2603.20260v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The integration of Large Language Models into Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) has enabled the so-lution of complex, long-horizon tasks through collaborative reasoning. However, this collec-tive intelligence is inherently fragile, as a single logical fallacy...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, API, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Domain-Specialized Tree of Thought through Plug-and-Play Predictors
item.description: arXiv:2603.20267v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have advanced complex reasoning, prominent methods like the Tree of Thoughts (ToT) framework face a critical trade-off between exploration depth and computational efficiency. Existing ToT implementations often rely o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, model, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FactorSmith: Agentic Simulation Generation via Markov Decision Process Decomposition with Planner-Designer-Critic Refinement
item.description: arXiv:2603.20270v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generating executable simulations from natural language specifications remains a challenging problem due to the limited reasoning capacity of large language models (LLMs) when confronted with large, interconnected codebases. This paper presents Factor...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> alignment, large language model, arxiv, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Me, Myself, and $\pi$ : Evaluating and Explaining LLM Introspection
item.description: arXiv:2603.20276v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A hallmark of human intelligence is Introspection-the ability to assess and reason about one's own cognitive processes. Introspection has emerged as a promising but contested capability in large language models (LLMs). However, current evaluations oft...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, large language model, arxiv, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LLM-Enhanced Energy Contrastive Learning for Out-of-Distribution Detection in Text-Attributed Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2603.20293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-attributed graphs, where nodes are enriched with textual attributes, have become a powerful tool for modeling real-world networks such as citation, social, and transaction networks. However, existing methods for learning from these graphs often a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, model, tool, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Leveraging Natural Language Processing and Machine Learning for Evidence-Based Food Security Policy Decision-Making in Data-Scarce Making
item.description: arXiv:2603.20425v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Food security policy formulation in data-scarce regions remains a critical challenge due to limited structured datasets, fragmented textual reports, and demographic bias in decision-making systems. This study proposes ZeroHungerAI, an integrated Natur...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, arxiv, framework, transformer, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Deep reflective reasoning in interdependence constrained structured data extraction from clinical notes for digital health
item.description: arXiv:2603.20435v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Extracting structured information from clinical notes requires navigating a dense web of interdependent variables where the value of one attribute logically constrains others. Existing Large Language Model (LLM)-based extraction pipelines often strugg...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, large language model, arxiv, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MARLIN: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Incremental DAG Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2603.20295v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Uncovering causal structures from observational data is crucial for understanding complex systems and making informed decisions. While reinforcement learning (RL) has shown promise in identifying these structures in the form of a directed acyclic grap...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, paper, RAG, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Probing the Latent World: Emergent Discrete Symbols and Physical Structure in Latent Representations
item.description: arXiv:2603.20327v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Video world models trained with Joint Embedding Predictive Architectures (JEPA) acquire rich spatiotemporal representations by predicting masked regions in latent space rather than reconstructing pixels. This removes the visual verification pathway of...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, vision, arxiv, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hybrid Autoencoder-Isolation Forest approach for time series anomaly detection in C70XP cyclotron operation data at ARRONAX
item.description: arXiv:2603.20335v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Interest Public Group ARRONAX's C70XP cyclotron, used for radioisotope production for medical and research applications, relies on complex and costly systems that are prone to failures, leading to operational disruptions. In this context, this stu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, product, research, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, context, prompt engineering, context window | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, retrieval, augmented | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, model, retrieval, knowledge base, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, platform, prompt, vector, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: CRoCoDiL: Continuous and Robust Conditioned Diffusion for Language
item.description: arXiv:2603.20210v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) provide an efficient non-causal alternative to autoregressive generation but often struggle with token dependencies and semantic incoherence due to their reliance on discrete marginal distributions. We address these limi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, arxiv, model, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: JointFM-0.1: A Foundation Model for Multi-Target Joint Distributional Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2603.20266v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite the rapid advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI), Stochastic Differential Equations (SDEs) remain the gold-standard formalism for modeling systems under uncertainty. However, applying SDEs in practice is fraught with challenges: modeling...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, zero-shot, model, API | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Transformer-Based Predictive Maintenance for Risk-Aware Instrument Calibration
item.description: arXiv:2603.20297v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate calibration is essential for instruments whose measurements must remain traceable, reliable, and compliant over long operating periods. Fixed-interval programs are easy to administer, but they ignore that instruments drift at different rates ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, transformer, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Bounded Coupled AI Learning Dynamics in Tri-Hierarchical Drone Swarms
item.description: arXiv:2603.20333v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern autonomous multi-agent systems combine heterogeneous learning mechanisms operating at different timescales. An open question remains: can one formally guarantee that coupled dynamics of such mechanisms stay within the admissible operational reg...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, embedding | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: fastapi_mcp - Expose your FastAPI endpoints as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, with Auth!

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, reasoning, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: RedacBench: Can AI Erase Your Secrets?
item.description: arXiv:2603.20208v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern language models can readily extract sensitive information from unstructured text, making redaction -- the selective removal of such information -- critical for data security. However, existing benchmarks for redaction typically focus on predefi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, arxiv, model, research, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Graph-Aware Text-Only Backdoor Poisoning for Text-Attributed Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2603.20339v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many learning systems now use graph data in which each node also contains text, such as papers with abstracts or users with posts. Because these texts often come from open platforms, an attacker may be able to quietly poison a small part of the traini...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, platform, paper, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, prompt engineering, step-by-step, tool, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: AgentComm-Bench: Stress-Testing Cooperative Embodied AI Under Latency, Packet Loss, and Bandwidth Collapse
item.description: arXiv:2603.20285v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Cooperative multi-agent methods for embodied AI are almost universally evaluated under idealized communication: zero latency, no packet loss, and unlimited bandwidth. Real-world deployment on robots with wireless links, autonomous vehicles on congeste...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, ICL, arxiv, experiment, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Compression is all you need: Modeling Mathematics
item.description: arXiv:2603.20396v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human mathematics (HM), the mathematics humans discover and value, is a vanishingly small subset of formal mathematics (FM), the totality of all valid deductions. We argue that HM is distinguished by its compressibility through hierarchically nested d...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, analysis, library, compression | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Collaborative Adaptive Curriculum for Progressive Knowledge Distillation

item.title: Interpretable Multiple Myeloma Prognosis with Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership Data
item.description: arXiv:2603.20341v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Machine learning (ML) promises better clinical decision-making, yet opaque model behavior limits the adoption in healthcare. We propose two novel regularization techniques for ensuring the interpretability of ML models trained on real-world data.
